modules.summary: The Login module provides a credential form that validates Email/Username and Password against the registered-user database and handles successful or failed authentication responses.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the initial sign-in entry point shown to visitors before reaching the Accounts Overview dashboard; upon successful authentication it redirects users into the app where the left-hand navigation (Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, etc.) becomes available. It sits at the start of the user session lifecycle, gating access to all subsequent modules.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Visitor is not authenticated and is presented with the sign-in page (no active user session).

modules.summary: A pre-authentication sign-up form that collects personal, contact, and credential data with client-side formatting and validation and creates a new customer account upon successful submission.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is part of the pre-login flow reachable from the sign-in page via the Register link and precedes access to the post-login left-hand navigation (Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, etc.). It operates before account access and hands off to the Login module after successful account creation.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated visitor who selected the Register link from the sign-in page and intends to create a new account.

modules.summary: Displays a personalized dashboard listing all customer accounts with masked account numbers, account details, status badges, and a total balance footer.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the default dashboard view reached immediately after a successful login and is accessible via the left-hand navigation as Accounts Overview among options like Open New Account, Transfer Funds, and Security Settings. It serves as the primary landing page showing an overview of a customer's accounts and balances within the application's main lifecycle after authentication.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with permission to view the customer's account data.

modules.summary: A form-based feature that creates a new Checking or Savings account by selecting an account type, specifying an initial deposit and funding source, and performing real-time validations on inputs and balances.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Open New Account' after successful sign-in, this module is part of the account onboarding flow in the dashboard and, on success, redirects users back to the Accounts Overview to show the new account.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated and on the dashboard with at least one existing funding account available for the initial deposit

modules.summary: Module for initiating fund transfers either between the user's ParaBank accounts or to an external account, including validation of amounts and account numbers and displaying success or error messages.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Transfer Funds' after successful sign-in and arrival at the Accounts Overview/dashboard; it is used during regular account operations to move money within the user's accounts or to external accounts and occurs after account selection in the session lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has at least one active Checking or Savings account available to select as a source; for external transfers the user may provide an external account number to confirm.

modules.summary: Processes bill payments by collecting payee and payment details, validating entries (including matching account numbers and available funds), executing the payment, returning a confirmation reference, and updating account balances.
modules.where_it_fits: This module is the application's Bill Pay screen accessible from the left-hand navigation after successful log-in (from the account dashboard). It is part of post-login account management features alongside Transfer Funds, Open New Account, and Payments-related tasks.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user on the account dashboard with at least one active source account (and sufficient funds for the intended payment)

modules.summary: Provides an interface to apply for Personal, Auto, or Home loans with validation of amounts, down payments, collateral, and a simulated credit decision.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under "Request Loan" after successful sign-in, this module is part of the post-login financial services allowing users to initiate new loan requests alongside other actions like Transfer Funds and Open New Account. It sits in the application lifecycle at the point where authenticated customers request credit and receive an immediate simulated approval or denial outcome.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has at least one existing account eligible to be selected as a collateral account with sufficient displayed balance

modules.summary: Editable customer profile form for viewing and updating personal contact details with validation and success/error feedback.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as 'Update Contact Info' (item ⑥) after successful login and landing on the account dashboard; it is part of the post-authentication account management flow for maintaining personal information.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and on the account dashboard with an existing customer profile whose contact fields are available to be loaded and edited.

modules.summary: Provides card issuance requests and card-control settings so customers can request new debit or credit cards and update spending limits, travel notices, and card status.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation after successful log-in via the "Manage Cards" menu option, this module is part of post-login account management functionality alongside Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, and other account services. It is used during the account lifecycle when customers need to request new cards or manage existing card controls.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with at least one existing account eligible for card linkage

modules.summary: Provides a portfolio snapshot and workflows for executing same-day fund trades and creating recurring investment plans, with input validation and confirmation messages.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation (Investments, item ⑧) after successful login and landing on the account dashboard; it is the application area for managing brokerage-style holdings and scheduling contributions as part of account management features.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in customer session with at least one linked funding/destination account and investment privileges (existing holdings or funding account available).

modules.summary: Provides generation of account statements for a selected account and period and management of e-statement (paperless) preferences including email validation.
modules.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the Account Statements option (menu item ⑨) after successful sign-in; it is part of the post-login account management features alongside Accounts Overview, Transfer Funds, and other account services. This module is used when users need transaction histories or to update paperless delivery settings within their account lifecycle.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is signed in and has at least one active account associated with their profile

modules.summary: Provides a form for authenticated users to change their account password by verifying the current password, enforcing a strong-password policy, and updating credentials with validation feedback.
modules.where_it_fits: Available from the left-hand navigation after successful login under Security Settings (reachable from the Account Dashboard / Accounts Overview). It is part of the account management and security lifecycle, used to maintain credential integrity for signed-in customers.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with an active authenticated session and has access to the account dashboard/navigation.

modules.summary: Support Center provides authenticated customers with a secure message form (subject, category, rich-text message, optional attachment) and a schedule-callback form (reason, preferred date/time window, phone) with inline validation and success confirmations including ticket IDs and email confirmation.
modules.where_it_fits: Available from the left-hand navigation menu after login as the 'Support Center' (item 11), this module is part of post-authentication customer support features accessed from the account dashboard and sits alongside other account management functions like Security Settings and Account Statements.
modules.assumed_state_on_entry: User is an authenticated/customer who has successfully logged in and reached the account dashboard
